forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXJWGS1DPP92870X79GPX96T
+- 01KXJWH0ZGPEAKRDWN520P15XY
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8480'
+title: read file operation
+---
+## What
+Per plan.md ┬¦3 op table row 1. Create `Sources/FileTool/Operations/ReadFile.swift` ŌĆö `@Generable @Operation` struct on `FileContext`:
+- Params: `path` (req), `offset?` (1-based line, Ōēż 1,000,000), `limit?` (> 0, Ōēż 100,000), `format?` (`hashline` | `plain`, default `hashline`)
+- Pipeline: PathGuard validate ŌåÆ read full file, UTF-8-or-reject (binary ŌåÆ corrective message, never decoded) ŌåÆ window (offset/limit over lines) ŌåÆ hashline tag with **absolute** start line
+- Output `ReadResult: Encodable { hash, lines, note? }` ŌĆö `hash` is the whole-file token over full on-disk bytes regardless of windowing; `note` reports the window ("showing lines 60ŌĆō120 of 843")
+- Bound violations (offset > 1M, limit 0 or > 100k, unknown format) ŌåÆ corrective messages naming valid values
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `#hash` token identical whether reading whole file or any window
+- [ ] Anchors carry absolute line numbers under offset (line 60 tags as `60:HH|`)
+- [ ] Binary file rejected in both formats with a corrective message
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/ReadFileTests.swift`: offset/limit/both; each bound violation; anchors absolute under windowing; token = full-file under window; plain opt-out (no anchors, no per-line tags); binary rejection both formats; empty file; unicode content; missing path corrective
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter ReadFileTests` ŌĆö expect: green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
